vulnerabilities.description: Sensitive data stored in plaintext JSON file without encryption. Any secret values set via memory.set() are persisted unencrypted at .claude-flow/data/memory.json.
Session context data stored in plaintext JSON files without encryption. Session state containing potentially sensitive context is written unencrypted to .claude-flow/sessions/
No input validation on memory keys. Arbitrary key names could potentially be used for state manipulation, though impact is limited to application-level state.
No access control on session context values. Any caller can read/write arbitrary session state via update() and get() methods.
localStorage used without user consent notice. Todo data stored in browser localStorage which is accessible to any JavaScript on the same origin.

recommendations: Encrypt sensitive data at rest in memory.js using crypto module (e.g., AES-256-GCM) before writing to .claude-flow/data/memory.json
Add encryption layer to session.js for storing context data, especially if session context contains authentication tokens or API keys
Implement access control checks in memory.js: validate key names against a whitelist or prevent sensitive prefixes (e.g., '_secret', 'apiKey', 'password')
Add documentation to app.js about localStorage security implications and implement user consent if storing sensitive todo data becomes necessary
Audit all uses of execSync in statusline.js and other helpers: verify they don't accept untrusted input; github-safe.js shows good practice with execFileSync
No hardcoded secrets detected, no SQL injection risk (no database), no XSS vulnerabilities in provided code—focus remediation on data storage encryption
Consider adding file permission checks to ensure .claude-flow/ and .claude-flow/sessions directories are readable only by the current user (chmod 700)
